blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I was at the table. Almost the whole time.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I got up once. To refill my drink.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I was at the table the whole time. I didn't move.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I went to the bathroom once. Around ten.
They went quiet. Going through their own memories. The way you do when a normal evening suddenly requires accounting.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: I was here. I stepped out to get some air — the back porch — for a few minutes. Maybe five, maybe ten. Then I came back in.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: I didn't notice you go out.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: It wasn't a thing. I just needed air.
A small silence. Not accusatory — just the silence of people filing information away without knowing why yet.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: So everyone had a gap. Somewhere in the evening. Even if it was small.
He didn't say it like an accusation. He said it the way you state a fact when the fact is inconvenient.
